data.title: The NFL Draft gives prediction markets another event to create waves
data.description:
The NFL Draft is scheduled to take place in Pittsburgh on Thursday night and run through the weekend. The high-profile fan event will give both sports betting apps and prediction markets a fresh burst of business. Sportsbooks should see a seasonal bump from draft props and first-round markets, while prediction markets are emerging as a broader, more accessible alternative that can capture draft interest in states where traditional betting is limited.

Notably, both Kalshi (KALSHI) and Polymarket (POLYMARKET) are offering significantly more NFL draft contracts than a year ago. Kalshi's (KALSHI) draft page shows draft markets split across multiple categories, including draft category, matchups, and over/under formats, while Polymarket (POLYMARKET) lists 159 live draft markets and 20 live NFL draft markets.

Analysts have noted that the NFL Draft sets up similarly to the recent Masters golf tournament, which saw unexpectedly heavy trading volume due to the changing leaderboard. The expectation is the player and pick trades could create the same dynamic that steers volume toward prediction markets. 

Sports betting volume saw a slump in February, but a strong rebound in March tied to increased interest in the NCAA basketball tournament. Following an uneven year for FanDuel (FLUT), DraftKings (DKNG), and BetMGM (MGM) (GMVHF), the World Cup in June and July is anticipated to give the industry a major boost against a soft comparable to a year ago.

Of note, the Roundhill Sports Betting & iGaming ETF (BETZ) is down 10% on a year-to-date basis.

data.title: Sox crosses 10,000 for the first time as chip rally broadens
data.description:
The PHLX Semiconductor Index surged past the 10,000 mark for the first time on record, marking a symbolic milestone for a sector that has become the backbone of the global equity rally.

The move reflects a powerful combination of AI-driven demand, improving memory cycles, and a rebound in semiconductor capital expenditure. 

According to TradingView data, the index gained over 1.8% on the session, extending a multi-year bull run that has consistently outpaced broader benchmarks.

Crucially, this is not a narrow rally. Gains have been distributed across the semiconductor value chain, from chip designers to foundries and equipment makers, highlighting the depth of the current upcycle.

Earnings revisions have trended higher, particularly in AI-linked segments, while supply-side constraints continue to support pricing power in advanced nodes and high-bandwidth memory.

The index composition underscores that breadth:

  • NVIDIA (NVDA) — $4.91T
  • Broadcom (AVGO) — $2.02T
  • Taiwan Semiconductor (TSM) — $1.69T
  • ASML (ASML) — $558.86B
  • Micron Technology (MU) — $541.27B
  • Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) — $498.79B
  • Intel (INTC) — $337.39B
  • Lam Research (LRCX) — $331.24B
  • Applied Materials (AMAT) — $327.01B
  • Texas Instruments (TXN) — $251.33B

While the breakout reinforces semiconductors as the market’s structural growth engine, valuations remain elevated, leaving the index increasingly sensitive to any moderation in AI spending or global demand.

Artificial Intelligence/Robotics ETFs: (AIQ), (BOTZ), (DTEC), (WTAI), (XAIX), (WISE), (GINN), (ROBT), (TECB), (XT), (THNQ), and (CHAT).

data.title: Warner Bros. Discovery shareholders approve Paramount Skydance merger
data.description:
• Warner Bros. Discovery (WBD) shareholders approved the merger with Paramount Skydance (PSKY) at the special stockholders meeting Thursday morning.
  • "With Paramount, we look forward to creating an exceptional combined company that will expand consumer choice and benefit the global creative talent community,” said Warner Bros. board chairman Samuel Piazza.
  • Paramount Skydance (PSKY) will pay $110.9B for the entire company—or ~$31 per share—to acquire all of its assets, including its movie studio, CNN, HBO, and the company's content library
  • The compensation package to Warner Bros. CEO David Zaslav was not disclosed.
  • The transaction is expected to close in the third quarter of this year, subject to customer closing conditions.

data.title: IBM's 'prudent' guidance faces higher expectations from investors, say analysts; stock tumbles
data.description:
Shares of IBM (IBM) tumbled about 9% on Thursday after the company's “prudent” guidance faced expectations of upward pressure from investors, according to the analysts.

IBM's CEO Arvind Krishna said on CNBC that the global economy is very resilient and that the company is seeing very good growth across geographies. Krishna added that it was prudent not to raise guidance given headwinds. IBM's mainframe business grew 48%, and the company is also seeing good progress on quantum, according to Krishna.

Krishna had said on the earnings call that "we continue to make progress in quantum and remain on track to deliver the first, large-scale fault-tolerant quantum computer by 2029."

Wedbush kept its Outperform rating but lowered the price target on the stock to $320 from $340.

"IBM reported its FY1Q26 results featuring beats on the top and bottom lines while maintaining its outlook for FY26 across revenue and free cash flow as the company takes a prudent approach to navigating the macro headwinds despite the strong beat while seeing stronger AI adoption demand within software services. We maintain our OUTPERFORM rating while lowering our price target from $340 to $320 reflecting a lower multiple to account for ~$600M of Confluent dilution and near-term consulting softness," said analysts led by Dan Ives.

IBM's CFO James Kavanaugh said on the earnings call that "Given where we are in the year, we believe it is prudent to maintain our guidance even as the underlying performance and execution are off to an encouraging start."

The analysts noted that IBM maintained its full year 2026 revenue growth of more than 5% in constant currency, or CC, as it plans to focus more on its software vertical, which is expected to grow 10% year-over-year in 2026 "as the company maintains a prudent approach to guidance due to the ripple effects of a difficult macro." IBM noted that macro uncertainties may cause people to spend less on enterprise projects, creating greater hesitancy to increase its guidance, the analysts added.

"We continue to believe IBM remains well-positioned to ride the wave of AI demand as its diversified portfolio and offerings gain traction while its productivity flywheel provides opportunities to expand margin while investing in innovation," said Ives and his team.

Morgan Stanley maintained its Equal-weight rating on IBM's shares but increased the price target to $225 from $215.

"Tonight's report was largely in-line with how we previewed the quarter - a modest 1Q beat, a 2Q guide largely in-line with expectations, and an unchanged full year outlook. Big picture, there's nothing wrong or bad about that outcome, especially given the uncertainty of how conflict in the Middle East plays out. But we believe there was investor expectation that we could see upward pressure on full year estimates that didn't materialize this quarter," said analysts led by Erik Woodring.

BNP Paribas has a $245 price target on IBM's stock.

"The company does not historically raise FY guidance at 1Q, but this may have been viewed as a disappointment as we estimate the early closing of Confluent should have added ~70bps to FY26 Software segment growth, and ~30bps to group growth, vs. the mid-May close scenario originally considered in guidance. FY26 Software growth guidance was raised to ‘10%+’ from ‘~10%’ previously. The company also reiterated guidance for 1pt of pre-tax margin expansion in FY26, and to add ~$1bn of FCF," said analyst Stefan Slowinski.

Update: The story was updated with IBM's CEO Arvind Krishna's comments.

data.title: U.S. natural gas inventory net change of +103 Bcf for week ended April 17 – EIA
data.description:
• Stocks for the week ended April 17 were 2,063 Bcf vs. 1,960 Bcf (revised from 1,970 Bcf) for the week ended April 10.
  • Net change: +103 Bcf vs. +59 Bcf for the week ended April 10.
  • Consensus: +96B
  • Natural Gas Futures (NG1:COM) -2.8% to $2.64/MMBtu.
  • ETFs: (UNG), (BOIL), (KOLD), (FCG), (UNL), (HNU:CA)
  • Click here to read the full EIA Weekly Natural Gas Storage Report.

data.title: Muncy Columbia Financial announces 3-for-1 stock split
data.description:
• Muncy Columbia Financial (CCFN), parent of Journey Bank, said on Thursday that its board has declared a 3-for-1 stock split of its outstanding common shares, effective April 23, 2026.
  • The board approved a stock split in the form of a 200% stock dividend on outstanding common shares.
  • Shareholders of record as of May 7, 2026, will receive two additional shares for each share held, payable after the close of business on May 14, 2026.
  • Following the distribution, total shares outstanding are expected to be 10.61M on a split-adjusted basis, net of treasury shares.
  • Shares +7.66%.

data.title: Robust earnings potential in tech, financials, industrials, and utilities – Wells Fargo’s Scott Wren
data.description:
The S&P 500 (SP500) closed at a record high despite a “roller coaster ride” of headlines in 2026, according to Scott Wren, senior global market strategist at Wells Fargo.

The strategist noted that while geopolitical tensions in the Persian Gulf—including a U.S. Navy seizure of an Iranian-flagged cargo ship and threats of Iranian retaliation—grabbed financial market attention, his team remains focused on sectors of the market where they see robust earnings potential and attractive valuations.

On April 6, Wren downgraded the energy sector (XLE), (AMLP), (VDE) from neutral to unfavorable and upgraded information technology (VGT), (XLK), (IYW) from neutral to favorable, guiding investors to reallocate from one sector to the other.

According to the strategist, while energy has gained nearly 23% year-to-date, the tech sector’s price-to-earnings ratio has fallen to a level more in line with the broader S&P 500 (SP500), and its consensus earnings growth estimate is nearly double that of the index.

Beyond technology, Wren maintains a “most favorable” rating on the financial sector (XLF), (VFH), (IYF). He also holds “favorable” ratings on the industrials (XLI), (VIS), (FXR), and utilities (XLU), (VPU), (FUTY) sectors, the strategist said, reflecting areas where his analysis identifies strong fundamentals ahead.

The strategist sees a similar rebalancing opportunity within commodities, he said. As oil prices (CL1:COM), (CO1:COM) surged during the opening weeks of the Iran war, international buyers were forced to sell precious metals like gold (GLD), (IAU), (SGOL) to raise dollars for higher-priced oil purchases, Wren explained.

He believes “an opportunity exists to reallocate from energy-related commodities into our favored precious metals segment of commodities.”

Wren also addressed broader market anxieties around artificial intelligence spending and private credit stability.

He does not “think extrapolating AI to put entire industries out of business will help portfolios,” the strategist said, adding that problems among mainly smaller private credit firms should not be extrapolated into a systemic concern.

data.title: Teck Resources surges as Q1 earnings beat on record copper sales, higher prices
data.description:
Teck Resources (TECK) up 6.3% in Thursday's trading to a multi-year intraday high of $63.19 after  easily beating expectations for Q1 adjusted earnings and revenues, bolstered by high commodity prices, record quarterly copper sales volumes, and stronger revenues from by-products.

Q1 profit attributable to shareholders jumped to $819M, or $1.67/share, from $370M, or $0.73/share, in the year-earlier quarter; adjusted EBITDA of $2.09M compared to $927M in the same quarter last year; and revenues of $3.94B, up 72% from $2.29B a year ago.

Teck (TECK) said its copper prices ‌averaged $5.83/lb in Q1, up from $4.24/lb a year earlier, as average copper prices in the quarter surged 36% ​from a year earlier and hit all-time highs in ​January.

Q1 copper sales ⁠surged 46% Y/Y to a company record 155K tons, and copper production jumped 32% to 140K tons, due to increased output at Quebrada Blanca mine in Chile, higher throughput and higher grades at Highland Valley Copper in Canada, and higher grades at Antamina in Peru.

Production at QB increased 31% to 55.5K tons as ​operations were ramped up, and Teck (TECK) maintained its full-year production guidance for the mine at 200K-235K tons; the asset is pivotal to Teck's tie-up with Anglo American, with QB set to be integrated with Anglo's nearby Collahuasi operation to form a larger asset complex capable of producing an additional 175K tons/year during 2030-49.

Teck (TECK) warned of higher fuel costs for its Chilean operations, as the global mining industry copes with supply-chain disruptions sparked by the Middle East war.

While fuel supply is not a major threat, "there could be an amplified impact on costs at our Chilean operations due to the requirement for diesel imports," the company said.

data.title: PulteGroup Q1 earnings miss as home closings, selling prices continue to fall
data.description:
PulteGroup's (PHM) Q1 earnings fell short of the average analyst estimate on Thursday, while its revenue declined less than expected as challenging housing market dynamics continued. Net new orders increased 3% Y/Y in the quarter, but the average sales price fell. Its gross margin was impacted by increased incentives offered to homebuyers.

Separately, the homebuilder said it increased its stock buyback authorization by $1.5B, bringing the current share repurchase authorization to $2.1B.

Q1 GAAP EPS of $1.79, trailing the average analyst estimate of $1.81, dropped from $2.57 in the year-ago period.

Q1 total revenue of $3.41B, beating the $3.38B consensus, declined from $3.89B in Q1 2025. Homebuilding sales of $3.34B fell from $3.80B a year ago.

PulteGroup (PHM) stock gained 0.5% in Thursday morning trading.

Home closings fell 7.3% Y/Y to 6,102, with the average selling price dipping to $542K from $573K in Q4 2025 and $570K in last year's Q1

Net new orders increased 3% Y/Y to 8,034, with a value of $4.57B.

Its cancellation rate, as a percentage of beginning backlog, was 13%, up from 11% a year ago.

"Within a demand environment impacted by domestic and global dynamics, we see a consumer with concerns about affordability and the economy but still desirous of home ownership, as demonstrated by the 3% growth in our first quarter net new orders," said President and CEO Ryan Marshall. "Given these dynamics, we continue to intelligently manage sales, incentives, and production to best position the Company for near- and long-term success."

Unit backlog was 10,427 homes with a value of $6.5B at the end of Q1, vs. 8,495 homes valued at $5.27B at the end of Q4 2025.

Home sales gross margin of 24.5% fell from 27.5%, reflecting higher incentives offered to buyers to reduce its excess speculative inventory.

Q1 selling, general, and administrative expenses declined to $380.3M from $393.3M in Q1 2025.

PulteGroup (PHM) reaffirmed its 2026 guidance of 28.5K-29.0K home closings, 24.5%-25.0% gross margin, and $1.0B of operating cash flow. The guidance still assumes the average sales price is $550K-$560K.

data.title: Biggest stock movers Thursday: TSLA, IBM, QS, NOW, and more
data.description:
Stock futures inched lower Thursday premarket, weighed down by persistent Middle East tensions despite President Trump's announcement of a ceasefire extension with Iran.

Here are some of Thursday's biggest stock movers:

Biggest stock gainers

  • United Rentals (URI) +20% - Shares climbed after delivering a solid earnings beat in Q1 and raising its full-year outlook, reinforcing demand strength. The company lifted its FY2026 revenue guidance to $16.9B–$17.4B and boosted adjusted EBITDA expectations to $7.63B–$7.88B, signaling continued momentum across construction and industrial activity.
  • Texas Instruments (TXN) +16% - Shares jumped after delivering a strong Q1 beat and upbeat guidance, signaling improving demand trends. The company reported EPS of $1.68 vs. $1.37 estimates and revenue of $4.83B (+19% Y/Y), driven by robust analog sales and strength in industrial and data center markets. Looking ahead, TXN guided Q2 EPS to $1.77–$2.05 and revenue to $5B–$5.4B, both well above consensus, reinforcing confidence in a cyclical recovery and sustained momentum.
  • QuantumScape (QS) +13% - Shares surged following Q2 results as it highlighted improved profitability and expanding demand beyond autos, with growing interest in its solid-state battery tech from military, aerospace, and government sectors. The company also pointed to a new opportunity in AI data center power infrastructure, where higher energy density could enable greater compute capacity. Progress on its Eagle Line and QSE-5 cell production further reinforced confidence in scaling, shifting the narrative from execution risk to broader commercial potential.
  • Mobileye Global (MBLY) +10% - Shares were in focus after posting a Q1 beat, raising its full-year outlook, and announcing a $250M share buyback, signaling confidence in its growth trajectory. The company now sees 2026 revenue at $1.94B–$2.02B (vs. the prior $1.9B–$1.98B) with adjusted operating income of $185M–$235M, reinforcing improving fundamentals and capital return support.

Biggest stock losers

  • ServiceNow (NOW) -14% - Shares tumbled despite strong Q1 results, as a weaker-than-expected margin outlook overshadowed solid growth. The company beat on revenue ($3.77B, +22% Y/Y) and matched EPS while raising both Q2 and full-year subscription revenue guidance, with remaining performance obligations jumping 25%. However, full-year subscription gross margin guidance of 81.5% came in below expectations, driven by acquisition-related dilution, and weighed on investor sentiment. Additional concerns around delayed large deal closures in the Middle East added to the pressure, shifting focus from top-line strength to near-term profitability.
  • Altimmune (ALT) -13% - Shares plunged after pricing an upsized, oversubscribed $225M public offering of common stock, pre-funded warrants, and accompanying warrants. The deal includes 64.25M shares and warrants exercisable at $3.00 per share, signaling near-term dilution concerns despite strong demand.
  • International Business Machines (IBM) -11% - Shares fell despite a solid Q1 beat, as unchanged full-year guidance disappointed investors. The company reported EPS of $1.91 and revenue of $15.92B (+9.5% Y/Y), with strength across software and mainframe-driven infrastructure, alongside better-than-expected free cash flow. However, management reiterated its outlook for ~5% constant-currency revenue growth, roughly in line but slightly below expectations, signaling limited upside despite a strong start. The lack of a guidance raise kept pressure on valuation multiples, overshadowing otherwise steady execution.
  • Super Micro Computer (SMCI) -8% - Shares dropped after a report from BlueFin Research claimed it lost a “significant” server contract with Oracle Corporation ((ORCL)), potentially worth $1.1B–$1.4B, though the claim remains unverified. The report also linked the situation to ongoing legal issues involving a co-founder tied to alleged export violations of NVIDIA Corporation chips to China, adding to investor concerns around governance and compliance risks. The combination of potential order loss and legal overhang weighed on sentiment, pushing the stock lower.
  • Tesla (TSLA) -3% - Shares fell as investors looked past the Q1 beat and focused on the company’s heavier spending plans and execution risks. Tesla signaled more than $25B in combined 2025–2026 capex, with Optimus production targeted for late July or August and Robotaxi expansion aimed at a dozen states by year-end, underscoring a long investment cycle before meaningful monetization. The company also disclosed a potential $2 billion stock-based acquisition of an AI hardware firm, with much of the deal tied to performance milestones, adding to concerns around near-term cash use even as Tesla continues to highlight its autonomy and robotics roadmap.
